methods.description: The spacing analysis was conducted to measure the spacing of macroscopic faults and deformation bands in the mapping area, using scan-lines and image analysis to understand the distribution and spacing of these geological features.

steps.description: The location, dip and dip direction, heave and throw, where possible, and shear-sense were recorded, and faults plotted onto a map.
steps.searchable_summary: Record and plot fault characteristics onto a map

steps.description: The spacing of macrofaults was measured along scan-lines oriented perpendicular to the average fault strike using the ruler tool in ImageJ.
Spacing greater than 20 m was removed from the data as these points represent artefacts of exposure conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter out spacing measurements greater than 20 m

steps.description: Horizontally measured spacing was corrected for the average dip of the faults using the sine transformation.
steps.searchable_summary: Correct spacing measurements using sine transformation

steps.description: Pearson correlation coefficient values were calculated for the correlation between the corrected spacing and the associated cumulative field overprinting criteria.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Pearson correlation coefficient for spacing data

steps.description: For spacing measurements of deformation bands in damage zones, the fault was put into the origin of the digitised image.
steps.searchable_summary: Set fault as origin in digitized image for spacing measurements

steps.description: Deformation band clusters were treated as single bands. The resulting maps were exported as a binary image.
steps.searchable_summary: Export deformation band maps as binary images

steps.description: Matlab was used to obtain spacing statistics along scan-lines at a spacing of 1% of the image height.
steps.searchable_summary: Use Matlab to calculate spacing statistics

steps.description: When the horizontal image dimension was not perpendicular to the traces of the deformation bands in the map, spacing was again corrected with the sine transformation.
steps.searchable_summary: Correct spacing with sine transformation for non-perpendicular images

steps.description: When normalised spacing is reported for damage zones, normalisation was done by division through damage-zone width.
steps.searchable_summary: Normalize spacing by dividing by damage-zone width

steps.description: Synthetic images were created to show ideal spacing distributions and to highlight how natural variation in heterogeneous rocks and data collection error can impact the measured spacing of natural samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Create synthetic images for ideal spacing distributions

methods.description: Microstructural analysis was performed on thin-sections of host rock and deformation bands using BSE imaging to examine porosity, mineralogy, and microfracture density.

steps.description: Twenty polished thin-sections from samples of host rock and deformation band were examined by back-scattered electron (BSE) imaging using a VP Zeiss Sigma scanning-electron microscope (SEM).
steps.searchable_summary: Examine thin-sections using BSE imaging with SEM

steps.description: Images of host rock were taken at 400x magnification and those of deformation bands at 800x. Multiple images were taken with a 5-10% overlap and stitched together to produce ca. 1 mm2 image.
steps.searchable_summary: Capture and stitch BSE images at specified magnifications

steps.description: BSE maps were analysed for porosity and mineralogy of the samples. Grey-level slicing was used to extract four different phases from the images.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze BSE maps for porosity and mineralogy using grey-level slicing

steps.description: For porosity phase analysis, the images were eroded and dilated to generate upper and lower bounds for the estimate.
steps.searchable_summary: Erode and dilate images for porosity phase analysis

steps.description: Grain size estimates were obtained through manual tracing of grains from SEM images in ImageJ.
steps.searchable_summary: Trace grains in SEM images to estimate grain size

steps.description: Microfracture density was measured on BSE maps along 1-mm long scan-lines oriented normal to the deformation bands.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure microfracture density along scan-lines on BSE maps
